description: Create a GitLab CI pipeline with a build‑stage job named generate that uses the Docker image registry.gitlab.com/datadrivendiscovery/images/testing:ubuntu-bionic-python36, sets the variable GIT_SUBMODULE_STRATEGY to recursive, excludes the branches dist and dev-dist, and runs the exact commands: set -o errexit; echo "Installing dependencies"; pip3 install -r requirements.txt; echo "Generating"; python3 -m autogenerate.wrap_sklearn --dir autogenerate/resources/primitive-jsons --overlays autogenerate/resources/overlay_extras.json autogenerate/resources/overlay_classifiers.json autogenerate/resources/overlay_regressors.json autogenerate/resources/overlay_preprocessors.json --module_config autogenerate/config/setup.config.json --primitive_id_file autogenerate/d3m_sklearn_wrap_ids.json --hyperparams_to_tune autogenerate/resources/hyperparams_to_tune.yml; echo "Running tests"; ./run_tests.sh 10; find . -name '*.pyc' -or -name __pycache__ -prune -exec rm -rf {} ';' and a conditional block that, when the commit ref is master or devel and GIT_ACCESS_USER/TOKEN are present, clones the project, checks out a dist or dev‑dist branch, copies the generated package, commits and pushes it if there are changes. The job publishes the path autogenerate/d3m_sklearn_wrap as an artifact. Add a deploy‑stage job named primitive-json that uses the Docker image registry.gitlab.com/datadrivendiscovery/images/libs:ubuntu-bionic-python36-stable, runs only on the master branch (also excluding dist and dev-dist), and executes the exact commands: set -o errexit; ls; echo "Generating primitive jsons"; a conditional block that clones common‑primitives, installs it with pip3 -e ., clones the current project, checks out the appropriate branch, installs the project, runs python3 -m autogenerate.generate_primitive_jsons, and if on master also clones the primitives repository, creates a JPL branch, moves generated JPL files, commits and pushes them; finally it stores the autogenerate directory as an artifact.
